If an evil thought should arise in your mind, suppress it. Watchfulness in habits is like a bridle on the head; watchfulness in actions is like a hand on the bridle. When you are not able to separate from wicked people, remember they will watch your words and twist them, if they can, to your disadvantage. Sometimes it may be necessary to keep silence, even from good words; but generally we are wrong when slow to engage in helpful conversation. Impatience is a sin that has its cause within yourself, which is dwelling on things; and its effects on yourself are no less than burning. In our greatest health and prosperity, every person is completely meaningless; he cannot live long; he may die soon. This is an undeniable truth, but we are very unwilling to believe it. Therefore let us pray that God would enlighten our minds by his Holy Spirit and fill our hearts with his grace, so that we may be ready for death every day and hour.

There is no lasting satisfaction to be found in created things; but it is found in the Lord and in fellowship with him; to him you should be driven by your disappointments. If the world is nothing but vanity, may God deliver us from having or seeking our portion in it. When reliance on others fails, it is comforting that we have a God to go to, a God to trust in. You may see a good God doing all and ordering all events concerning you; and a good person, for that reason, says nothing against it. He desires the forgiveness of his sin and the prevention of his shame. We must both watch and pray against sin. When under the correcting hand of the Lord, we must look to God himself for relief, not to anyone else. Our ways and our actions bring us into trouble, and we are beaten with a rod of our own making. What a poor thing is beauty! And what fools are those who are proud of it, when it will certainly, and may quickly, be gone! The body of people is like a garment for the soul. In this garment sin has lodged a moth, which wears away first the beauty, then the strength, and finally the substance of its parts. Whoever has watched the progress of a lingering illness or the work of time alone on the human frame will immediately feel the force of this comparison, and that surely every person is vanity. Afflictions are sent to stir up prayer. If they have that effect, we may hope that God will hear our prayer. The believer expects weariness and mistreatment on his way to heaven; but he will not stay here long: walking with God by faith, he goes forward on his journey, not diverted from his course or discouraged by the difficulties he meets. How blessed it is to be unattached to things here below, so that while going home to our Father’s house, we may use the world without being controlled by it! May we always look for that city, whose Builder and Maker is God.
